commentaries.author: John Chrysostom
commentaries.content: (a) But even if they would needs bring over the Gentiles to their side, they learn that neither must the Apostles overlook it. And the Apostles and elders, etc.

commentaries.author: Tertullian of Carthage
commentaries.content: But there rose up certain of the sect of the Pharisees who believed, saying, It is needful to circumcise them, and to command them to keep the law of Moses. Tertullian of Carthage Of this epistle, how that certain persons interposed, and said that men ought to be circumcised, and that the law of Moses was to be observed; and how the apostles, when consulted, determined, by the authority of the Holy Spirit, that "a yoke should not be put upon men's necks which their fathers even had not been able to bear.
